figures.caption: Table 6: Ablation study performed on REG dataset.

Figure 2: Example qualitative result for pathology report generation. From left to right, the figure shows the whole-slide image thumbnail, the report generated by the proposed model, and the corresponding ground-truth report.

Figure 1: End-to-end framework for our concept-grounded pathology report generation. The proposed framework integrates multi-scale histopathology information and curated clinical concepts to generate coherent and interpretable pathology reports. WSIs and pathology concepts constitute the primary inputs (left). Patch-level visual features are extracted using a frozen CONCH[21] encoder, slide-level representations are obtained via a TITAN[7] encoder, and concept embeddings are derived using GECKO[15] (Feature Extraction). These modality-specific features are first aligned through lightweight adaptive projection networks before being processed by a Context-aware Encoder. The encoder performs iterative depth-aware encoding, where self-attention is modulated via feature-wise linear modulation (FiLM[27]) to condition patch representations on global slide context and expert-derived concepts, enabling progressive refinement across layers. Layer-wise outputs are aggregated to form a unified multimodal representation. A Contextadaptive Decoder then applies learned gated fusion and cross-attention mechanisms to dynamically integrate patch-, slide-, and concept-level features during text generation, producing structured and clinically meaningful pathology reports (right).
